units.title: My home
units.subtitle: Rooms, things in your room, and where they are — aligned with Cambridge Prepare Lv1 Unit 3.
units.grammar_focus: there is / there are; prepositions (in/on/under/behind/next to); have got (intro).

prepare_alignment.source: Cambridge Prepare 2nd Edition Level 1 — Unit 3 'My home' (SB p24-27)

steps.instruction: Look at Lucia's house. Match the room.

items.definition: The room with a bath, shower and toilet.
items.example_sentence: I brush my teeth in the bathroom.

items.definition: The room where you sleep.
items.example_sentence: My bedroom is small.

items.definition: The room where you eat at a table.
items.example_sentence: We eat dinner in the dining room.

items.definition: The space outside with plants and grass.
items.example_sentence: The cat is in the garden.

items.definition: The room you enter from the front door.
items.example_sentence: Hang your coat in the hall.

items.definition: The room where you cook food.
items.example_sentence: Mum is in the kitchen.

items.definition: The room with a sofa where you watch TV.
items.example_sentence: We watch TV in the living room.

games.instruction: Match each picture with the right word.
Listen to the word and tap the right picture.
Look at the picture and type the word.

steps.title: Lucia's house
steps.text: Hi! I'm Lucia. This is my house. We have seven rooms. There is a kitchen. We cook in the kitchen. There is a living room with a big sofa. There is a dining room with a long table. We have two bathrooms — one upstairs and one downstairs. There is a bedroom for me and a bedroom for my brother. We have a small garden too. I love my home!
steps.scene_description: A two-storey house with seven rooms labeled.

questions.question: How many rooms does Lucia's house have?

steps.explanation: We use 'there is' (or short 'there's') to talk about ONE thing in a place. 'There is a kitchen' = a kitchen exists in this place. For more than one we use 'there are' (next lesson).
examples: There is a sofa in the living room.
There's a bed in my bedroom.
There's a mirror on the wall.
There is a cat in the kitchen.

items.sentence: There ___ a sofa in the living room.
There ___ a cat in the kitchen.
There ___ a sofa in the living room.
There ___ a cat in the kitchen.
There is a sofa in the living room.
There's a bed in my bedroom.
There's a mirror on the wall.
There is a cat in the kitchen.

steps.audio_text: TOM: Hi Lucia! Welcome to my new house. LUCIA: Wow, Tom! It's big. How many rooms? TOM: There are five. Look — there's a kitchen here. LUCIA: It's nice. Is there a garden? TOM: Yes, there's a small garden at the back. LUCIA: And a dining room? TOM: No, we eat in the kitchen. But there is a living room. Come and see!
steps.scene_description: Tom shows Lucia his new house.

steps.prompt: Describe your house. Use 'There is...' for 3 rooms.
steps.expected_text: There is a kitchen. There is a living room. There is a bedroom for me.
prompts: Pick 3 rooms: kitchen / bedroom / bathroom / living room / garden.
Add one detail: 'It's big.' or 'It's small.'

questions.question: Choose: ___ is a kitchen in my house.
Where do you eat at a table?
True or false: 'living room' is where you watch TV.

steps.instruction: Look in Tom's bedroom. What can you see?

questions.question_text: What is the thing you sleep on?
Where do you put your books?

items.definition: A thing in your bedroom where you sleep.
items.example_sentence: My bed is big and soft.

items.definition: A thing with four legs you sit on.
items.example_sentence: The chair is at the desk.

items.definition: An electronic thing you work and play on.
items.example_sentence: My computer is on the desk.

items.definition: A wooden box with doors for storing things.
items.example_sentence: The plates are in the cupboard.

items.definition: A table where you do homework.
items.example_sentence: My desk is near the window.

items.definition: A small light on a table or wall.
items.example_sentence: Turn on the lamp, please.

items.definition: A flat thing that shows your face.
items.example_sentence: I look in the mirror in the morning.

items.definition: A flat board on a wall for books or things.
items.example_sentence: There are books on the shelf.

games.instruction: 60 seconds! Tap the right picture for each word.
Read the word and tap the right picture.
Flip the cards and find the pairs.

steps.title: Your Rooms! — Tom's bedroom
steps.text: Hi! I'm Tom. This is my bedroom. There is a bed and there is a desk for my homework. There is a computer on my desk. There are two chairs — one at the desk and one near the window. There are three shelves with my books. There is a small lamp on the desk. There are two mirrors and a big cupboard for my clothes. My room is busy but I love it!
steps.scene_description: A teenager's bedroom with desk, computer, shelves of books, and two chairs.

steps.explanation: When there is MORE THAN ONE thing in a place, we use 'there are'. 'There are two chairs' = two chairs exist here. The thing after 'there are' is always plural — add -s (chairs, shelves, books).
examples: There are five books on the desk.
There are four students in the room.
There are two mirrors on the wall.

items.sentence: There am five books on the desk.
There am four students in the room.
There am two mirrors on the wall.
There are five books on the desk.
There am four students in the room.
There are two mirrors on the wall.
There ___ five books on the desk.
There ___ four students in the room.
There ___ two mirrors on the wall.

steps.audio_text: MUM: Tom, where are you? TOM: I'm in my bedroom, Mum. MUM: What are you doing? TOM: I'm doing my homework. MUM: Are there books on your desk? TOM: Yes, there are five books. And there's a computer. MUM: Is there a lamp on? TOM: Yes, there is. The lamp is on my desk. MUM: Good. Dinner is in ten minutes!
steps.scene_description: A mum and son talk through the door.

steps.prompt: Describe your bedroom. Use 'There is...' and 'There are...'.
steps.expected_text: There is a bed and a desk. There are two chairs and a computer.
prompts: More than one: 'There are two chairs.'
Where it is: 'on the desk', 'next to the bed'.

questions.question: True or false: 'shelves' is the plural of 'shelf'.

steps.instruction: Look at Alex's messy room. Where are the things?

questions.question_text: The book is on the desk. The cat is ___ the bed.

items.definition: A long soft seat for two or three people.
items.example_sentence: The sofa is in the living room.

items.definition: A flat thing with legs you put things on.
items.example_sentence: The book is on the table.

items.definition: A screen for watching shows.
items.example_sentence: The TV is in the living room.

items.definition: A big cupboard for your clothes.
items.example_sentence: My clothes are in the wardrobe.

items.definition: A big paper picture you put on the wall.
items.example_sentence: I have a poster of a footballer.

items.definition: Inside something.
items.example_sentence: The cat is in the box.

items.definition: On top of something.
items.example_sentence: The book is on the table.

items.definition: Below something.
items.example_sentence: My shoes are under the bed.

items.definition: At the back of something.
items.example_sentence: The ball is behind the door.

items.definition: Beside something.
items.example_sentence: My chair is next to the desk.

games.instruction: Listen to the word, then type it.
Listen and tap the right word.

steps.title: Alex's busy room
steps.text: Hi! I'm Alex. My room is small but it has lots of things! There is a bed in the corner. There is a wardrobe next to the bed. The wardrobe is big — my clothes are in it. There is a sofa under the window. There is a TV on a small table. My football poster is on the wall behind the bed. My school bag is under the desk. And my cat? She is on the sofa! She loves my room too.
steps.scene_description: A small bedroom with bed, wardrobe, sofa, TV, poster, and a cat on the sofa.

steps.rule_pattern: Prepositions — in, on, under, behind, next to
steps.explanation: Prepositions tell us WHERE something is. 'in' = inside. 'on' = on top of. 'under' = below. 'behind' = at the back of. 'next to' = beside. We put the preposition before the place: 'The cat is on the sofa.'
examples: The cat is on the sofa.
My books are in the bag.
The ball is under the chair.
The poster is behind the bed.
The lamp is next to the bed.

items.sentence: The lamp ___ next to the bed.
The lamp ___ next to the bed.
The cat am on the sofa.
My books am in the bag.
The ball am under the chair.
The poster am behind the bed.
The lamp am next to the bed.

steps.audio_text: ALEX: Where is my phone? MUM: Is it on your desk? ALEX: No, it isn't. MUM: Is it in your bag? ALEX: No, my bag is under the desk and the phone is not in it. MUM: Look behind the sofa. ALEX: Yes! It's here, behind the sofa. Thanks, Mum! MUM: Always behind something! Look next time.
steps.scene_description: Alex can't find his phone and Mum helps.

questions.question: Is the phone on the desk?

steps.prompt: Hide something in your room (real or imagined). Tell a friend where it is using 3 prepositions.
steps.expected_text: My bag is under the desk. My phone is on the chair. My book is next to the lamp.
prompts: Pick a preposition: in / on / under / behind / next to

questions.question: The cat is ___ the bed (below it).
A long soft seat in the living room is a ___.
True or false: 'My clothes are in the wardrobe' means inside the wardrobe.

lessons.title: My home — Review
lessons.topic: Recycle rooms + things + prepositions. have got introduction.

steps.instruction: Look at three different rooms. Whose is whose?

questions.question_text: It has a sofa, a TV and a small table. It's a...
It has a bed, a wardrobe and a desk. It's a...

games.instruction: Climb the ladder! Choose the right word for each step.
90 seconds! Race through all the words you learned.

steps.title: Three rooms
steps.text: Look at three friends' rooms. Lucia's room is big. There is a bed and a wardrobe. There are two posters on the wall. Tom's room is busy. There is a desk with a computer. There are three shelves with books. There are two chairs. Alex's room is small. There is a bed in the corner. The cat is on the sofa under the window. Three friends, three different rooms — but they are all happy with their rooms.
steps.scene_description: Three teenagers' bedrooms side by side.

patterns: there is + singular (a kitchen, a bed)
there are + plural (two chairs, three books)
Prepositions: in, on, under, behind, next to
have got — introduction (I have a desk. She has a computer.)

items.sentence: there is + singular (a kitchen, a bed)
there am + plural (two chairs, three books)
Prepositions: in, on, under, behind, next to
have got — introduction (I have a desk. She has a computer.)

steps.audio_text: GUIDE: Welcome to our house! Let me show you. This is the hall. VISITOR: Lovely. GUIDE: There's the kitchen on the right. We cook there. There are three windows. VISITOR: And the living room? GUIDE: It's here, next to the kitchen. There is a big sofa and a TV. VISITOR: Are there bedrooms upstairs? GUIDE: Yes, there are four bedrooms and two bathrooms. And we have a small garden at the back. VISITOR: It's a beautiful home!
steps.scene_description: A guide shows a visitor around a house.

questions.question: How many windows in the kitchen?

steps.prompt: Welcome a friend to your home. Describe 2 rooms in 30 seconds.
steps.expected_text: This is the kitchen. There is a fridge and a table. This is the living room. There is a sofa and a TV.
prompts: Move to room 2: 'And this is the [room].'

questions.question: There ___ five rooms in my house.
The cat is ___ the sofa (on top of).
A long seat for 2-3 people is a ___.
True or false: 'There is a TV.' is about ONE TV.
